mnemonics: Block Party Invitation: A block matrix's eigenvalues are invited from each block's characteristic party, but the minimal polynomial is the LCM of the minimal after-party plans.

D.I.Y. (Determinant In Blocks, Yay!): For a block diagonal matrix, the characteristic polynomial is the product of the individual block's characteristic polynomials. Just multiply them together yourself!

Minimal is the Least: The minimal polynomial of a block diagonal matrix is the least common multiple (LCM) of the minimal polynomials of its diagonal blocks. It's the 'least' complicated shared schedule.
keyPoints: For a block diagonal matrix, the characteristic polynomial is the product of the characteristic polynomials of the diagonal blocks: p_A(λ) = p_A₁₁(λ) * p_A₂₂(λ) * ... * p_Aₖₖ(λ).
The spectrum (set of eigenvalues) of a block diagonal matrix is the union of the spectra (sets of eigenvalues) of its diagonal blocks.
The minimal polynomial of a block diagonal matrix is the least common multiple (LCM) of the minimal polynomials of its diagonal blocks.
commonMistakes: Assuming the eigenvectors of the full matrix are simply the concatenated eigenvectors of the blocks. While the block's eigenvectors (padded with zeros) are eigenvectors, the full matrix can have eigenvectors that are combinations from different blocks.

Incorrectly applying the rules for block diagonal matrices to block triangular matrices. The eigenvalues are still the union of the diagonal block eigenvalues, but the characteristic polynomial is not simply the product.
Forgetting that the LCM for the minimal polynomial must be taken over the polynomials themselves, not just their degrees or roots, to ensure it annihilates every block.
